question: On a business rule task with a called DMN decision, when are the input mappings applied to transform variables for the decision engine?

options.text: Only once, on the initial activation of the task.
On activating the task, and again before each re-evaluation when an incident at the task is resolved.
After the decision is evaluated, just before the `resultVariable` is merged.
Only when an output mapping is also defined at the same task.

a.text: Input mappings are not a one-shot operation. They are also reapplied whenever an incident at the task is resolved, before the decision is re-evaluated.

b.text: Input mappings are applied on activating the business rule task, and again when an incident at the task is resolved before the decision is re-evaluated. This can affect the new decision result.

c.text: Input mappings run before the decision evaluation, not after it. Post-evaluation handling is governed by the `resultVariable` and output mappings.

d.text: Input and output mappings are independent. Input mappings still run on activation and on incident resolution even if no output mapping is configured.

explanation: Input mappings on a business rule task run on activating the task (before the decision is evaluated) and are reapplied whenever an incident at the task is resolved. Because mappings re-execute on resolution, a fresh set of variables can change the decision result.
